task_description: # RedwoodSDK: React 19 Metadata Tags ## Background A RedwoodSDK project is pre-installed at `/home/user/myapp`. React 19 hoists `` and `<meta>` components into the document head automatically. Use that mechanism so each page renders its own page-specific metadata, while the `Document` shell stays generic. ## Requirements Implement page server components that render `<title>` and `<meta>` tags inline. Do **not** hard-code page-specific titles in the `Document` component. ## Acceptance Criteria - Project path: /home/user/myapp - Start command: npm run dev -- --host 0.0.0.0 --port 5173 - Port: 5173 - Routes (all under the existing `render(Document, ...)` block): - `GET /` → final HTML `<head>` must contain `<title>Home — rwsdk` and `Home`. - `GET /about` → `` must contain `About — rwsdk` and `About`. - `GET /docs` → `` contains `Docs — rwsdk`, `Docs`.
truth:
# Verification Plan ## Setup 1. cd /home/user/myapp 2. Start the dev server: `npm run dev -- --host 0.0.0.0 --port 5173`. 3. Wait until port 5173 is listening. ## Verification Steps 1. `GET /` → 200; body contains `Home — rwsdk`, contains `Welcome to the rwsdk demo home page.`, contains `

Home

`. 2. `GET /about` → 200; body contains `About — rwsdk`, contains `Learn more about the rwsdk demo.`, contains `

About

`. 3. `GET /docs` → 200; body contains `Docs — rwsdk`, contains `Docs for the rwsdk demo.`, contains `/og/docs.png`, contains `

Docs

`.
